provenance.title: Very Deep Convolutional Networks for Large-Scale Image Recognition

summary: Systematic evaluation of CNN depth on accuracy. Proposes VGGNet: configurations A–E (11–19 weight layers) using small 3×3 convolutional filters throughout. Achieves state-of-the-art ILSVRC classification and localization results. Demonstrates that depth is key to improving accuracy.

modules.name: ConvBlock3x3
modules.description: Repeated 3×3 conv + ReLU, channels fixed within block

modules.name: MaxPooling2x2
modules.description: 2×2 max pool with stride 2 after conv blocks

modules.name: FullyConnected
modules.description: Three FC layers (4096, 4096, 1000) with ReLU and dropout (0.5)

equations.desc: Effective receptive field, L layers, stride s

training.data_augmentation: Random 224×224 crops from rescaled images, horizontal flips, RGB color jittering

assumptions: All weights initialized from random normal, biases from zero
LRN not used (found to not improve performance; we exclude per paper)
ambiguities: Exact number of training epochs with LR decay schedule not fully specified
Multi-scale testing details (Section 4.2) involve heuristic averaging
